scenario.summary: Month-end AR review — Continental, Pacific Dynamics past due
scenario.situation: End of month. The AR Manager reviews the outstanding receivables portfolio — $38M in open invoices across ~2,500 customers. DSO has crept up from 34 to 41 days over the past two quarters. The CFO flagged this in the last leadership meeting. Two accounts are particularly concerning: Continental Mfg AG (EUR 890K, 22 days past due) and Pacific Dynamics ($415K, 31 days past due with no communication). Meanwhile, Apex Heavy Equipment ($780K) is due today and historically pays 5-7 days late.
agentSteps: Builds a risk-scored collection priority queue across all open invoices — by payment probability based on historical behavior, credit profile, and external signals

Continental Mfg AG: flags as high risk — payment history shows deterioration (avg 15 days late to 22 days late over 6 months), recommends immediate outreach with escalation to their CFO and tightening credit terms

Pacific Dynamics: flags as critical — 31 days overdue with no payment activity or communication, credit rating dropped from B to B-, recommends hold on pending $180K order and initiating formal collection
Apex Heavy Equipment: predicts payment will arrive by day 5 based on historical pattern (87% of payments land between day 32-37), auto-schedules reminder
Titan Automotive: healthy — current, consistent payer, no action needed
Generates a DSO improvement plan: if Continental and Pacific Dynamics are resolved, DSO drops from 41 to 36 days
scenario.expectedOutput: Risk-scored collection queue with recommended actions per account, predicted payment dates for current invoices, DSO impact analysis, auto-generated outreach emails (draft) for high-risk accounts
